channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Could Oracle be about to take over TikTok? Trump reportedly backs plans for shock deal
item.description: Speculation mounts after President Trump signed an executive order to extend the deadline for a sale to American buyers.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: This weird mini PC has a huge built-in touchpad, and could be yours for just over $100
item.description: The KN1 mini PC touchpad supports multi-finger gestures for navigation, switching apps, and enhancing productivity in everyday tasks.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: AMD exec hints that discrete RDNA 4 GPUs won’t be in gaming laptops anytime soon, leaving Nvidia’s RTX 5000 cards unchallenged
item.description: If you were hoping RDNA 4 GPUs might be powering up gaming laptops anytime soon, I’ve got some bad news for you.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A Critical Microsoft Exchange Server Flaw Remains Unpatched: A Serious Security Concern
item.description: A critical security vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange servers has been largely unpatched, despite a fix being issued four years ago. This flaw may have aided the hacking group Salt Typhoon, and experts are urging security teams to take action to secure their networks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chris Hayes: Musk changes to X 'massively destructive' to how people see the news
item.description: MSNBC host Chris Hays tore into billionaire Elon Musk and warned against what he characterized as the "massively destructive" effect he is having on the information business. "Oh, it’s just massively destructive.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Todo sobre los CIO 100 Awards Spain 2024
item.description: En la última edición de los CIO 100 Awards celebrados en España (el 11 de diciembre de 2024) resultaron premiados los ejecutivos Juan Antonio Relaño, de Bosch, y Olga Forné, de Abertis, como el mejor CIO y CISO del año, mientras que las distinciones a la mejor empresa y administración pública recaye
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Outsmart hackers who are out to steal your identity
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers tips on how to protect your identity: Be vigilant, monitor accounts, use smart security, know theft response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Global data consumption hit a major new high in 2024 - here's why
item.description: Global data consumption rose 15% year-over-year for 2024, and these two categories are largely responsible.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: CaixaBank Tech sumará a sus filas 500 desarrolladores durante el primer trimestre de 2025
item.description: La filial tecnológica de CaixaBank, CaixaBank Tech, ha anunciado formalmente la ampliación de su equipo profesional con la incorporación de 500 desarrolladores durante el primer trimestre de este recién inaugurado 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: El MIT suscribe un acuerdo con Generali para impulsar la transformación digital
item.description: El Instituto Tecnológico de Massachusetts (MIT, por sus siglas en inglés), una de las universidades más prestigiosas del mundo en investigación aplicada, ha suscrito un acuerdo de colaboración con Generali.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to pick the right SAST tool
item.description: Like pretty much any security tool, picking a SAST (static application security testing) tool isn’t a matter of “What’s the best?” but rather “What’s the best for me?” Whether you already have a tool that needs replacing or you’re starting from greenfield, it’s helpful to understand the market so yo
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Crooks can't steal this motorcycle that hides in plain sight
item.description: A nondescript metal box transforms into a fully functional electric motorcycle that blurs the line between vehicle and urban infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: File taxes online: Tools, tips, deadlines and more
item.description: It’s now the norm for Americans to file their taxes online — the IRS reports that more than 90% of individual tax returns were e-filed in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How dangerous are 3D printers? Maybe enough for a background check
item.description: As 3D-printed gun violence abounds, some lawmakers are looking to cut the problem at the root. The New York state senate is currently evaluating a bill that would dramatically change the 3D printing market.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Smile! You can now control your Chromebook with your face
item.description: Google has started rolling out a number of new features in Chrome OS, according to The Verge, including a new feature that allows a user to control their Chromebook using head movements (to move the mouse pointer) and facial expressions (to click or activate dictation).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI debuts AI agent Operator to transform web task automation
item.description: OpenAI has unveiled “Operator,” a new AI agent designed to perform web-based tasks, offering potential productivity enhancements for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A new Instagram feature might expose your embarrassing habits
item.description: Instagram Reels has added a new feature that shows you a feed of videos that your friends have liked. The bad news: It works both ways, meaning your friends can now see every video you’ve liked.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Massachusetts Democrats ask regulators to open Trump meme coins probe
item.description: Two Massachusetts Democrats sent a letter to various regulators asking them to open an investigation into President Trump's and first lady Melania Trump's recently launched meme coins. The letter, signed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) and Rep. Jake Auchincloss (D-Mass.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 81% of firms back a Zero Trust approach to cyber defense
item.description: A recent survey of cybersecurity professionals indicates that 81% of organizations are turning to zero trust as the foundation of their cybersecurity strategies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: State of JavaScript: Highlights of the JavaScript developer survey
item.description: Getting a complete picture of the multi-headed beast that is the JavaScript ecosystem isn’t easy, but the annual State of JavaScript survey is a good place to start. What makes this survey stand out is that it attracts the input of thousands of working developers and other JavaScript enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sustainability, grid demands, AI workloads will challenge data center growth in 2025
item.description: Data centers this year will face several challenges as the demand for artificial intelligence introduces an evolution in AI hardware, on-premises and cloud-based strategies for training and inference, and innovations in power distributions—all while opposition to new data center developments continu
item.dc:creator: Network World
